Typo – Please note that this is a Headword Typo!  This correction involves a few considerations: 1) the printed text reads: “(or -tā, f.)”.  Therefore, the Headword MUST BE CHANGED to "siddhatā"; 2) Furthermore, you may consider this to be some sort of grouping with the primary listing "siddhatva" ?  3) The Indentation of this word "siddhatā" may need to be changed in the Hierarchy List so as to match equal status with the Headword "siddhatva". It is generally accepted that neuter words ending in "-tva" and feminine words ending in "-tā" have EQUAL status in the category of derivation.

Typo – This is a suggested correction by the following reasoning: By virtue of the letter "ā", one is led to believe that the letter "Ch" also indicates itself. BUT actually the letter "Ch" is a layman's way depicting the letter "C" in Sanskrit. In other words "Ch" is the WRONG letter!  Now I know that Andhrabharati approves of such things because, "That is just how it is", but I prefer things to be Accurate, regardless of (wrong) usage.

Typo – This is a suggested correction by the following reasoning: By virtue of the letters "ṇ", "ḍ" and "ā", one is led to believe that the letter "Ch" also indicates itself. BUT actually the letter "Ch" is a layman's way depicting the letter "C" in Sanskrit. In other words "Ch" is the WRONG letter!  Now I know that Andhrabharati approves of such things because, "That is just how it is", but I prefer things to be Accurate, regardless of (wrong) usage. Also, such a spelling as "Chaṇḍāla" goes against Andhrabharati's own principal that "Different Modalities should NOT be mixed within a single Entity or Form etc.". That is a "paraphrase", but I am certain that Andhrabharati will catch my point. Therefore maintaining this spelling "Chaṇḍāla" is really a bit of a "Pickle" !  Therefore, I am making all such correction in my own dictionary!

column6: Please add the number "2" to the right side of this Headword in the hierarchy list to properly mark the location of this Homonym. This represents a unique case, whereby the supplemental entry from the annexure introduces a Homonym, which previously did not exist. Upon the insertion of this Supplemental word into the Main Entries, the Homonym simultaneously comes into existence and REQUIRES the numerical distinction to be added to the Window Display and to this Headword located within the Hierarchy List Column. You will see that some following Headwords also are Homonyms by the well known fact the pūrvapada "kara" Itself is a Homonym, thus generating compounds that are Homonyms as well, ALL of which use and require the numberings "1" and "2". For this particular case, I suggest introducing the number "2" In Front Of the "circle-S" symbol that indicates the Supplemental entry.
